Ducks have not moved back into the area, 500 - 2,500 ducks. Mallards currently make up about 60% of the ducks on the area, rest of the birds are mixed species. Around 10,000 - 25,000 geese, mostly snows, with maybe a 1,000 darks. Almost all the birds are using the refuge at this time.<br/><br/>If the report has not been updated in a few days, please assume that conditions and bird numbers are the same as the last update.<br/><br/>Water level<br/><br/>Pool 1A is 59", 1B and 1C are around 46", 2 has about 36", 3A has 22", 3B has 24", 4A has 26", 4B has 25", 5 has 30".<br/><br/>Currently about 75% frozen with very thin ice.<br/><br/>Hunting conditions<br/><br/>Pool 2: Good moist soil production in areas that were sprayed the last couple years and in areas that were free of cattails. About 200 acres of cattails was aerially sprayed on 8/1/18.<br/><br/>Pool 3A: All of the pool was aerially sprayed for cattails on 8/1/18. Like always it will be closed to motorized boats.<br/><br/>Pool 3B: 209 acres were disked twice and drilled to millet. Another 40 acres was no-till drilled to millet. Another 300 acres of cattails have been disked. Good moist soil production.<br/><br/>Pool 4A: Some moist soil production.<br/><br/>Pool 4B: 80 acres were disked twice and planted to millet. Another 38 acres were no-till drilled to millet. Good moist soil production.<br/><br/>Pool 5: All 1,400 acres were burned and disked at least 5 times. There are a few areas of cattails along the dike and in some deeper spots that are still too wet to disk. Pool 5 will be open for teal season.<br/><br/>Burned an additional 1,500 acres of uplands and small wetlands in Pool 5 and 3B.<br/><br/>Use the foot crossings along the roads in all pools<br/><br/>Expected hunting success<br/><br/>Fair. Very few ducks or geese are being harvested at this time. Success is highly variable depending on the day.<br/><br/>iSportsman (electronic daily hunt permit system) will be required of all hunters this fall. There will not be any cards available this year, so please set up an iSportsman account prior to this hunting season.<br/><br/>1. Scroll down the left side of the page to the blue "register" button.<br/><br/>2. Provide hunter information.<br/><br/>ONCE REGISTERED, HUNTERS WILL BE ABLE TO CALL OR LOG ONTO THE SYSTEM SEPTEMBER 1 AND GET THEIR PERMIT FOR THE DOVE OPENER. THEY CAN ALSO VISIT THE CHEYENNE BOTTOMS WILDLIFE AREA PAGE ON iSPORTSMAN AT <br/><br/>Kingman State Fishing Lake is full. All marshes are planted to Japanese Millet and are full.<br/><br/>Hunting conditions<br/><br/>Good<br/><br/>WARNING-New rip rap has been added to the Kingman State Lake dam and until the metal has been removed, it poses a hazard to hunters, fishermen, and their dogs.<br/><br/>Expected hunting success<br/><br/>Fair to good, but improving with any cold fronts that would move birds down. Pressure is heavy and many animals are being hazed to the point that they are using private water.<br/><br/>Comments<br/><br/>Hunters! Bids closed for the NAWCA wetland project as of 10/17/2017. We started moving dirt the week of November 13, 2017 and the north project was completed last winter. We will be flooding those marshes west and north of the lake with a shallow depth in order to not threaten the integrity of the dikes while perennial vegetation is being established. Also, a new kayak/canoe launch has been installed on the dam just west of the refuge line to aid launching boats across the rip rap. We will be adding a hardened surface beside it to better facilitate launching. The south part of the NAWCA project in the old Bison Marsh was not started in 2018 due to wet conditions, however, it will be flooded and the work put off until 2019.<br/><br/>Approximately 3,200 ducks were observed on 12/7/18. Primarily mallard but some goldeneye, teal, and mergansers were also present. 200 Canada geese. Additional ducks may be present within flooded vegetation at the upper ends of the lake, but are not easily viewed. Scouting is encouraged prior to your hunt.<br/><br/>Lake approximately 90% ice covered on 12/10/18. With forecasted temperatures, anticipate ice conditions to change daily. Habitat conditions are slightly improved as compared to most years. Food producing vegetation became established on lake fringes as a result of drought conditions and declining lake levels. In mid-August rains returned and gradually filled the lake in early September. Flooding in October and November has diminished the quality of some of this vegetation however.<br/><br/>Approximately 1200 ducks and 800 Canada geese observed on 12/04. Various species of puddle ducks and divers. Flooded vegetation has made observation of ducks difficult. Scouting encouraged prior to your hunt.<br/><br/>Water level<br/><br/>Approximately 0.52' above conservation elevation (1339') on 12/04.<br/><br/>Hunting conditions<br/><br/>Most of the summer the lake was approximately 4.5' below conservation pool. Good moist soil production of vegetation has occurred along the exposed shore line and mud flat areas throughout the summer due to the low water levels. With a fall raise in water level improved habitat conditions may enhance opportunities for waterfowl hunters.<br/><br/>ATTENTION: i-Sportsman isREQUIRED to hunt this area. All hunters MUST use the electronic daily hunt permit system! Please check in, report your harvest, and check out using i-Sportsman for each and every hunt.<br/><br/>Ice cover in the marsh was still near 100% earlier in the week, but some of the ice has been thawing the last couple of days. Based on the weather forecast, which indicates less frigid temperatures over the next week, the amount of open water should gradually increase as we near the end of the first split of duck season.<br/><br/>Duck and goose numbers are changing daily as weather conditions and ice cover change. As of Friday morning, there were approximately 1,000 ducks and 15,000 - 18,000 geese in the Big Basin refuge. Goose numbers are highly variable throughout the day as the geese trade back and forth from feeding.<br/><br/>See "Water Levels" below for more information.<br/><br/>Remember that all activities in the refuge areas areprohibited, AND THE NORTH REFUGE UNIT IS NOW IN EFFECT, along with the south refuge in the Big Basin.<br/><br/>Current Information<br/><br/>As of Friday the 7th, road conditions are good.<br/><br/>Water levels<br/><br/>Big Basin:Most public hunting marshes are full.<br/><br/>Chain of Lakes: Most public hunting marshes are full.<br/><br/>Little Sinkhole Unit: Most public hunting marshes are full.<br/><br/>Hunting conditions<br/><br/>Conditions will hopefully be stable after the October flood event. There should be plenty of water for the rest of the season!<br/><br/>Expected hunting success<br/><br/>Fair. Abundant water and food should have the ducks and hunters spread out a little more.<br/><br/>Comments<br/><br/>ATTENTION:<br/><br/>MPWL has transitioned to a new daily permit system called Isportsman. Hunters will need to register with i-Sportsman prior to hunting at MPWL. Hunters will login (or call) each day prior to hunting to get a daily permit, and report harvest similar to the paper daily permits. Please visit  to register prior to hunting season.<br/><br/>Kansas hunters should be aware that iSportsman Electronic Daily Hunt Permits are required on 25 wildlife areas, including McPherson Valley Wetlands (check the 2018 Kansas Hunting and Furharvesting Regulations Summary or kdwpt.isportsman.net for a list).<br/><br/>iSportsman replaces the old paper permits and allows hunters to conveniently check in and out using a telephone, smartphone or home computer. As with the old paper system, information gathered helps area managers tailor management practices specific to an area and hunter preference. The paper system was inconvenient for hunters and labor intensive for managers.<br/><br/>**THERE IS A "NO TRASH POLICY" IN EFFECT AT MCPHERSON VALLEY WETLANDS. PLEASE CARRY OUT WHATEVER YOU CARRY IN, INCLUDING EMPTY HULLS AND OTHER TRASH. PLEASE TRY TO LEAVE YOUR HUNTING SPOT AS CLEAN OR CLEANER THAN IT WAS WHEN YOU ARRIVED. THANKS!!!<br/><br/>REFUGE AREAS: THE SOUTH REFUGE AREA IN THE BIG BASIN IS OFF-LIMITS TO ALL ACTIVITIES, AND THIS INCLUDES DUCK HUNTING. THERE IS NO HUNTING OR PUBLIC ACCESS OF ANY KIND ALLOWED IN THE REFUGE!!<br/><br/>The Little Sinkhole/Farland Lake marsh unit is included in the EARLY low-plains duck zone (as is the rest of MPWL).<br/><br/>All shotgun hunters at McPherson Wetlands are required to use non-toxic shot for all hunting. This includes upland birds.<br/><br/>There are approximately 20 waterfowl present on the area at this time. Should be more coming in with this next cold front.<br/><br/>Mallard<br/>Migration Rank<br/><br/>Current rank:10<br/><br/>This rank is subjective and ranges from 0 (no mallards) to 10 (peak numbers)<br/><br/>Water level<br/><br/>All pools have water in them and are 50% ice covered.<br/><br/>Hunting conditions<br/><br/>Fair<br/><br/>Expected hunting success<br/><br/>Fair<br/><br/>Comments<br/><br/>SCWL is using a new daily permit system called Isportsman. Hunters will need to register prior to hunting at SCWL. Hunters will login (or call) each day prior to hunting to get a daily permit, and report back harvest similar to the paper daily permits. Please visit  to register prior to hunting season.<br/><br/>Current duck numbers are less than 100 with the main species being Mallard along with Gadwall. Goose numbers are close to 500, mostly Canada with a few snow geese mixed in.<br/><br/>Mallard Migration Rating<br/><br/>Current Rating=5 The rank is subjective and is from (0= No Mallards, 1=Very few Mallards, 5=Moderate numbers, 10= Peak numbers).<br/><br/>Water level<br/><br/>Lake level is currently 1349.66 (Conservation pool is 1350.50)<br/><br/>Hunting conditions<br/><br/>Good.<br/><br/>Lake levels have been below conservation pool for all of 2018. Some of the boat ramps are still closed.<br/><br/>There has been very good moist soil production on the shoreline and mudflats (yellow nut sedge, smartweed, barnyard grass), and this vegetation is starting to see significant flooding now that the water levels have started to rise. Crops in the areas surrounding the reservoir have been poor due to drought this year.<br/><br/>5% Ice coverage.<br/><br/>Expected hunting success<br/><br/>Fair.<br/><br/>Comments<br/><br/>Remember Marion Reservoir has ZEBRA MUSSELS. Make sure to Clean, Drain and Dry your equipment. Clean all visible vegetation and mud from decoys, bags, waders, etc. Drain water from decoy bags and boats. Allow equipment to Dry for a five day period before using it at another area, OR wash with high-pressure hot water. Many of the roads around the reservoir can get very greasy after rain, or melting snow, please use good judgement about which roads you try to travel on.<br/><br/>Small numbers of mallards, mixed duck species, and Canada geese were observed using the reservoir this weekAround 5,000 ducks have been observed around the lake and on the wetlands. The majority of the ducks observed were mallards. Around 10,000 snow geese are also using the reservoir.<br/><br/>Water level<br/><br/>The COE has been slowly dropping the reservoir water level over the past couple of weeks after a foot and a half rise. The water is currently a half foot above conservation pool but will continue to drop until they close the gates. GOOD WATER AMOUNTS IN WETLANDS.<br/><br/>Hunting conditions<br/><br/>GOOD VEGETATION EXCEPT WHERE REPEATED FLOODING HAS TAKEN A TOLL.<br/><br/>Expected hunting success<br/><br/>Fair to Good.<br/><br/>Comment<br/><br/>For updated waterfowl numbers and weather conditions call (620) 331-6820. Individuals utilizing the wildlife area need to be aware of the refuge boundaries. Maps can be obtained at the Fisheries & Wildlife office (below the dam) or at the State Park (on the lake side).<br/><br/>Small numbers of mallards and mixed duck species were observed using the reservoir this week. A few hundred Canada geese were seen using local ponds and harvested crop fieldsReservoir is still ice free, but expect it to start closing up in near future. Boats ramps are all open and accessible as of 12/07/18. Big and little marshes below dam are pretty much iced up on edges. Birds are scattered around lake, but concentrations can be found at major creek entrances, such as mouth of Otter Creek.<br/><br/>Lower marsh is currently 100% full, and consist of some millet and moist soil annuals. Upper Marsh below dam is 100% full. All department managed marshes are open for public waterfowl hunting, and are located below John Redmond Dam south of spillway. Beware, both the upper and lower marsh's contain some deep water in lower portions, well over 6 feet in depth. The large sedimentation basins located below damn are not open to public hunting and are under private ownership.<br/><br/>Expected hunting success<br/><br/>Average. If you have boat and are able to access remote areas of reservoir, especially timbered creeks out of the wind, I would expect to have above average success.<br/><br/>To see real time John Redmond elevations please visit---- <br/><br/>Comments<br/><br/>For information on Flint Hills National Wildlife Area and the marshes on the upper end of the lake please contact FHNWR @ 620-392-5553.<br/><br/>All hunters must check-in daily using iSportsman electronic check-in from a PC, smartphone, or by phone before hunting and check out at the end of the hunt regardless of harvest. We now have a zero tolerance policy on failure to comply with iSportsman and check station requirements. Please remember to fill out the check-out report Go to:<br/><br/>Outboard motors are restricted on this wildlife area and are allowed in Unit G and in the boat lane only in Unit A-East. Outboards are not allowed in any other wetland units. Electric trolling motors are unrestricted. Boats must be operated at wakeless speed. Boats must have sufficient PFDs for occupants.<br/><br/>Go to MDCWA and click the "News" icon near the top for more information on general waterfowl habitat. Waterfowl report is updated only if there are significant changes in waterfowl numbers or hunting conditions.<br/><br/>Waterfowl counts given on this report are based on what was observed on the day the report is updated. Waterfowl numbers vary greatly from day to day and weather conditions and hunting pressure will affect the number of waterfowl on the area.<br/><br/>Mallard Migration Rank = 1 (0 = no mallards and 10 = peak migration)<br/><br/>11/21/2018 ( 11:00 AM )---<br/><br/>3 Duck Marsh- Both units are pumped and full of water, 70 mallards.<br/><br/>Willow Marsh- North and South units are pumped and full. No waterfowl were observed at the time of the survey.<br/><br/>Sundance Marsh- No waterfowl were observed at the time of survey.<br/><br/>Lowman's Cove- Full of water.<br/><br/>Shoveler Slough- LOCATION: South parking lot for Shoveler Slough is at the intersection of W 317th St. & S Davies Rd. 100 mallards and 20 unknown ducks were observed at the time of this survey. (NOTE: Motorized boats are prohibited)<br/><br/>Refuge- Observed waterfowl at the time of the survey 150 mixed divers, largely Goldeneye. Along with approximately 75 Canada geese, 30 gadwall, and 25 mallards.<br/><br/>Japanese and German millet (50/50 mix) has been drilled in all 5 of our wetlands and is limited due to drought conditions throughout the summer. Millet as well as Milo was planted in alternating strips throughout all of our wetlands to provide varying structure heights as well as options for ducks to feed on moist soil vegetation and grains. Varying food sources an abundance of invertebrates should provide enough food for the ducks to hang around all season.<br/><br/>Expected hunting success<br/><br/>Fair<br/><br/>Comments<br/><br/>All hunters utilizing Melvern Wildlife Area are required to obtain a free daily hunt permit through the new electronic permit system. Hunters can register now to begin using the electronic system at  For more information(click here). Waterfowl hunters utilizing the main lake are only required to obtain a permit when hunting west of Hoch RoadSouth Unit- Dry and will remain dry for 2018-2019 seasons for renovation work (see "Area News").<br/><br/>For more information on the water level conditions for upcoming season please see the area news by clicking link below and clicking on the "NEWS" icon.<br/><br/>Hunting conditions<br/><br/>Good.<br/><br/>Pools are all full enough now to run both outboards and mud motors throughout pools 1, 2A and 2B. There are no motor restrictions on the area just be courteous to other hunters as you transverse through hunting pools.<br/><br/>This weeks weather looks like we could potentially freeze up or at least have >1/2 inch ice towards the weekend, so please use extreme caution as weather and conditions change.<br/><br/>Corn and millet production was exceptional this year. Moist soil seed production was some of the best we have seen on the area and we are extremely pleased. Habitat and water levels are excellent for this time of year. Please keep in mind that it is a long season and we will continually be staging water levels up throughout the season providing more food with each stage up.<br/><br/>Please see the area news page for a breakdown of habitat types within each pool.<br/><br/>Expected hunting success<br/><br/>Southeast Zone Regular Duck Season November 10, 2018- January 6, 2019 and January 12-27, 2018<br/><br/>Fair<br/><br/>Hunting continues to be fair. There will be ice developing later in the week so utilize extreme caution with changing weather conditions. Hunters have been averaging around 2 birds per hunter this season.<br/><br/>We still have hunters that have not checked out of the iSportsman system. Please check to make sure it is not you and please remember to check out by 11:59 daily. Thank You!!!<br/><br/>Comments<br/><br/>If the red check out sign is out at check station all hunters are required to stop. Thanks<br/><br/>Neosho WA requires all hunters to utilize the daily permit system called isportsman. Hunters will need to register via PC prior to hunting at Neosho WA. Hunters will be able to login via PC, smartphone, or call in from cell phone or landline phone each day prior to hunting to get a daily permit, and report back similar to the paper daily permits. Please visit  each individual hunter needs to check in/out and report his/her harvest totals. i.e., NOT A GROUP HARVEST TOTAL.<br/><br/>Waterfowl hunting in Pool 8 is restricted to youth hunters and their mentor on weekends and holidays. | Macaralele Sandhill sunt vânate în Kansas din 1993 și în Central Flyway din 1961. Vânătoarea este permisă de Serviciul de Pește și Faună Sălbatică al SUA în nouă din cele 10 state din Central Flyway. Deși unele persoane se opun vânătorii de macarale, vânătoarea de macarale se face pe o bază durabilă în cadrul unui plan de gestionare a populației aprobat de Serviciul de Pește și Faună Sălbatică al SUA. Vânătoarea sportivă este, de asemenea, justificată pentru menținerea distribuțiilor rezonabile ale macaralelor de nisip. Dacă concentrațiile devin prea mari în unele zone, poate apărea depredarea culturilor și crește riscul de îmbolnăvire a macaralelor. Vânătoarea de macarale este extrem de provocatoare, deoarece macaralele sunt foarte precaute, iar carnea de macara este considerată excelentă, probabil cea mai bună dintre toate păsările migratoare de vânătoare.<br/><br/>Macaralele de nisip care migrează prin Kansas, și uneori iarna aici, fac parte din populația Mid-continent. Această populație este una din nouă în America de Nord și de departe cea mai mare cu aproximativ 500.000 de păsări. Următoarea populație mai mare numără aproximativ 20-25.000 de păsări.<br/><br/>Reglementările de vânătoare au fost formulate și planurile de urgență utilizate pentru a minimiza șansa ca macaralele să fie împușcate accidental de către vânătorii de macarale de nisip. Deși macaralele de nisip încep să se concentreze în număr de vânători la sfârșitul lunii octombrie, începutul sezonului de vânătoare a fost amânat până miercuri după prima sâmbătă din noiembrie pentru a permite majorității macaralelor să emigreze prin stat. Ore de fotografiere pentru macarale Sandhill începe la răsăritul soarelui, mai degrabă decât o jumătate de oră înainte de răsăritul soarelui, pentru a minimiza șansa unui vânător de macarale Sandhill trage din greșeală o macarale whooping din cauza condițiilor de lumină scăzută. (Kansas este singurul stat din Central Flyway care a întârziat orele de tragere pentru a proteja macaralele care se învârt.) Planurile de urgență ale statului federal, Quivira National Wildlife Refuge și Cheyenne Bottoms Wildlife Area oferă îndrumări cu privire la acțiunile de întreprins în cazul în care macaralele sunt prezente în timpul sezoanelor de vânătoare.<br/><br/>Macaraua cu ochi - fotografie de Mike Blair, KDWP<br/><br/>Macaralele de nisip și păsările de apă trebuie să fie conștiente de faptul că macaralele de șuviță apar mai frecvent în Kansas, în special în zonele Cheyenne Bottoms și Quivira, și să poată identifica macaralele de șuviță. Macaralele whooping și macaralele Sandhill sunt similare în mărime și formă, dar macarale whooping sunt albe cu vârfuri negre. Pedeapsa pentru împușcarea unei macarale whooping este o amendă de până la 100.000 $ și / sau până la un an în închisoare. Pentru mai multe informații, vizitați:<br/><br/>Test online pentru Sandhill Crane Hunters - Acest test este obligatoriu pentru toți vânătorii de macarale Sandhill. Testul are o dimensiune de 378k și va dura câteva minute pentru a descărca dacă aveți un modem. Este necesar Flash 6, pe care majoritatea calculatoarelor îl au. Dacă nu, veți fi rugat să descărcați Flash 6.<br/><br/>Broșura macaralei whooping - Informații despre macaralele whooping și cum să le distingem de speciile care arată similar cu ele.<br/><br/>USFWS Informații pentru păsări de apă și macarale de nisip Kansas (Waterfolls și Sandhill Crane Hunters) Țintă de stat (Waterfolls și Sandhill Crane Hunters) - Scopul acestui site este de a oferi vânătorii de păsări de apă și macarale de nisip cu informații care vor reduce probabilitatea de a trage ilegal la păsări migratoare. (Cei care nu sunt obligați să aibă o licență de vânătoare Kansas includ oameni care își vânează propriile terenuri, și rezidenți 15 și mai mici)<br/><br/>NOU PENTRU 2016: Licența de vânătoare de păsări de apă de 48 de ore nu mai este disponibilă pentru vânătorii de păsări de apă KS, a fost înlocuită de Licența anuală de vânătoare.<br/><br/>Timbre și licențe pentru păsări de apă - Toate timbrele și licențele pentru păsări de apă pot fi achiziționate de la orice agent licențiat, Pratt Operations Office sau online, cu excepția timbrelor federale pentru păsări de apă, care sunt achiziționate de la o Poștă din SUA, oficiile regionale și parcului KDWPT și agenții de licență selectați. Toate timbrele de păsări de apă sunt bune pe tot parcursul sezonului.<br/><br/>Kansas HIP Timbrul: $2.50<br/><br/>Timbrul de stat al păsărilor de apă: 10 $<br/><br/>Timbru Federal de Păsări de Apă: $26.50 - Cumpărat la orice birou KDWP sau la orice Oficiu Poștal din SUA.<br/><br/>Dacă vânați Sandhill Crane, în plus față de o licență de vânătoare (dacă este necesar), este necesar un permis federal de vânătoare de macarale Sandhill eliberat prin și validat de Departamentul de Sălbăticie și Parcuri din Kansas pentru a vâna macarale. Permisul de macara obtinut in alte state nu este valabil in Kansas. Permisul validat (taxa de validare de $7.50) este disponibil la orice agent licențiat, online, Pratt Operations Office sau birou regional.<br/><br/>Toți vânătorii de macarale din Kansas trebuie să treacă testul pentru vânătorii de macarale din Sandhill înainte de a obține un permis de vânătoare de macarale din Sandhill.<br/><br/>"Test online pentru vânătorii de macarale Sandhill." Faceți clic pe acest link pentru a lua un test online care oferă informații despre identificarea macaralelor de tip whooping, macaralelor de tip Sandhill și a speciilor asemănătoare, precum și capacitatea vânătorului de a alege fotografii sigure. Testul este atât distractiv cât și informativ și durează doar câteva minute.<br/><br/>Rezervorul Sebelius este aproape de aceeași altitudine ca anul trecut. Lacul este de aproximativ 11 picioare sub piscina normală.<br/><br/>Condiții de vânătoare<br/><br/>Lacul a primit suficient aflux pe parcursul verii pentru a menține nivelul apei stabil. Există unele zone superficiale care ar trebui să ofere locuri pentru vânătoare. Există o mulțime de noroi pentru a prinde vânătorii - aveți grijă în timp ce mergeți de-a lungul țărmului.<br/><br/>Succesul așteptat la vânătoare<br/><br/>Vânătoarea de succes = Minim<br/><br/>Comentarii<br/><br/>Norton Reservoir se află în zona de înaltă plaiuri a doamnei<br/><br/>Jaluzelele pot fi folosite și trebuie construite din materiale naturale erbacee sau resturi lemnoase care sunt prezente pe site. Jaluzelele construite din materiale nenaturale trebuie îndepărtate din proprietate în fiecare zi. Vă rugăm să nu aduceți materiale din afara site-ului.<br/><br/>Reamintire - Refugiul este NU ACCES. Gâștii care au fost împușcați și aterizează în refugiu NU POATE fi recuperate. Vă rugăm să utilizați bunul simț și să alegeți fotografii care nu vor permite gâștelor împușcate să aterizeze în refugiu.<br/><br/>TOŢI VÂNĂTORII de la Glen Elder WA sunt obligaţi să verifice intrarea şi ieşirea zilnic. Acest regulament a intrat în vigoare la 1 septembrie 2017. Utilizatorii sunt încurajați să utilizeze sistemul de permise de vânătoare electronice ISportsman zilnice, dar pot utiliza, de asemenea, sistemul de telefon sau cele 3 stații de card de hârtie care sunt pe proprietate. Accesați pentru mai multe informații.<br/><br/>Vegetaţia inundată din nou, apa peste tot. Temperaturile mai reci încep să îngheţe zonele superficiale, poate că va trebui să spargă gheaţa la rampele bărcii.<br/><br/>Succesul așteptat la vânătoare<br/><br/>Corect, scăderea numărului de raţe.<br/><br/>Bun, numărul gâştelor de zăpadă a scăzut, dar încă mai sunt multe.<br/><br/>Comentarii<br/><br/>Zona Low Plains Early Duck se deschide din 13 octombrie până în 16 decembrie 2018. Această zonă este la nord de autostrada 24 și include zonele Granite și Oak Creek din zona Glen Elder Wildlife Area.<br/><br/>Zona de rață târzie Low Plains se deschide din 27 octombrie până în 30 decembrie 2018. Această zonă este la sud de autostrada 24.<br/><br/>Sezoanele de Gâscă întunecată și Gâscă ușoară sunt deschise de la 7 noiembrie 2018 până la 17 februarie 2019.<br/><br/>Sezonul White-Fronted Goose este deschis până pe 30 decembrie 2018. Se va redeschide de la 26 ianuarie până la 17 februarie 2019.<br/><br/>Atât Cawker City, cât și Granite Creek Refuge se închid între 1 noiembrie și 31 ianuarie.<br/><br/>Zona de vanatoare pentru tineri/mentori este situata in refugiul Granite Creek si este in vigoare de la 1 octombrie pana la 31 ianuarie. Vânătoarea de păsări acvatice din zona de coastă este permisă în zona mentorului de tineret atâta timp cât fiecare adult vânează cu un tânăr de 17 ani sau mai mic. Barcile de orice fel sunt interzise în refugiul Granite Creek.<br/><br/>Miezul de zebră a fost confirmat în lacul Waconda. Asigurați-vă că se usucă bărcile și echipamentele înainte de a se muta în alte ape.<br/><br/>Mai puțin de 200 de gâște negre și mai puțin de 100 de gâște negre observate pe 7/12/18.<br/><br/>Amintiți-vă: vânătorii sunt obligați să verifice și să verifice folosind sistemul electronic de permise de vânătoare zilnice iSportsman atunci când vânează zona.<br/><br/>Mallard Migration Rank<br/><br/>Ratingul curent - 1 Rank-ul este subiectiv și este de la (0= Fără Mallards, 1=Foarte puține Mallards, 5=Numere moderate, 10=Numere de vârf).<br/><br/>Nivelul apei<br/><br/>Ploile recente au creat o mulțime de gheață de mai puțin de un inch grosime, ceea ce face foarte periculos. Toate mlaștinile sunt pline sau inundate.<br/><br/>Condiții de vânătoare<br/><br/>Activitatea păsărilor de apă a fost extrem de lentă din cauza condițiilor de îngheț. 99% acoperire de gheață cu mai multe găuri mici păsările păstrează deschise.<br/><br/>Din cauza inundațiilor și a proiectului de construcție, drumul de vest care duce la zona de acces a bărcii din Gamekeeper Marsh de-a lungul Marsh Creek va fi închis. Cei care doresc să vâneze Gamekeeper Marsh la sud de linia refugiului vor putea să se lanseze pe rampa de est a barcii direct la nord de barajul Gamekeeper. Cu toate acestea, cu nivelurile scăzute ale apei, lansarea unei bărci va deveni mai provocatoare.<br/><br/>Navigatorii trebuie să se asigure că înregistrarea lor este actualizată și că au toate echipamentele necesare la bord și operaționale, inclusiv jachete de salvare și lumini de navigație.<br/><br/>Toate vehiculele trebuie să rămână pe drumurile desemnate și pe zonele de parcare.<br/><br/>Reamintire:<br/><br/>Toți vânătorii sunt obligați să facă check-in și check-out atunci când vânează zona.<br/><br/>Aceste informații importante colectate din permisele de vânătoare îi ajută pe manageri să adapteze practicile de management specifice unei zone și preferințelor vânătorului.<br/><br/>Refugiul este închis<br/><br/>Succesul așteptat la vânătoare<br/><br/>Cu gheață și nopți reci în prognoza, zona nu se va dezgheța până când nu vom vedea maxime în anii 30 și minime peste 25 de grade.<br/><br/>Rezervorul este la 1583,1 sau 0,5 picioare deasupra bazinului de conservare (1582,6) sau 0,5 picioare în po0l de inundații și căzând încet. Influențele de la Lovewell din White Rock Creek din cauza ploii vor continua să scadă spre sfârșitul acestei săptămâni. Debitele de la White Rock Creek au început și sunt la 250 cfs.. Rezervorul va fi tras în jos pentru a ajunge la obiectivul de elevație USBR iarna. Eliberările pot fi programate pe tot parcursul iernii pentru a menține apa la altitudinea de management de iarnă. Irigare de vară Drawdown a fost mai târziu în acest sezon, astfel încât nu prea multă vegetație pe noroi goale.<br/><br/>Condiția de vânătoare<br/><br/>Bun. Kyle East: S-au făcut renovări majore la această unitate a lui Kyle. Reconstrucţia cu pârghie, instalarea unei structuri de poartă cu şurub pentru a muta apa din est în vest, defrişarea copacilor/pensului prin întreaga unitate, semănarea mei şi a sorgului pe tot parcursul lucrărilor şi şanţurilor pentru îmbunătăţirea drenajului au fost finalizate. Kyle West: PISCIE COMPLETĂ Această unitate a fost uscată complet în acest an și a permis o cantitate enormă de vegetație umedă a solului nativ să crească, ceea ce va oferi cantități nesfârșite de hrană pentru rațe odată inundate. De asemenea, am împrăştiat mile japoneze în apartamentele de noroi ale unităţii de vest la începutul verii şi totul creşte bine. O bandă de barcă a fost construită pentru a face mai uşor de călătorie pentru bărci de la rampa la capătul sudic al unităţii. Ambele rampe de ambarcațiune vor fi, de asemenea, renovate și se va adăuga pietriș suplimentar pentru a facilita lansarea ambarcațiunii.<br/><br/>West River: Din nou, cu ajutorul Capitolului Leavenworth Ducks Unlimited am reușit să răspândim Millet și Sorghum atât în unitățile superioare, cât și în cele inferioare ale West River. O cantitate semnificativă de arbori / perii a fost efectuată atât în unitățile superioare cât și inferioare ale West River, împreună cu unele pulverizare cocklebur, plantarea sorgului și plantarea porumbului. Pomparea va continua în unitatea superioară, iar noi am experimentat inundații în unitatea inferioară, vom continua să adăugăm apă la unitatea inferioară pe măsură ce timpul progresează.<br/><br/>Ferguson superior: Această unitate a fost în mare parte uscată în primăvară / vară și nu a primit niciodată nici o ploaie suplimentară pentru a suplimenta. Cu condițiile extrem de uscate din această piscină, acest lucru a promovat unele coada-mare și albine, cu toate acestea, am obținut încă o recoltă bună de albine anuale și mei amestecate, de asemenea. Aceasta este mlaștina refugiului nostru și se închide 1 octombrie până la 15 ianuarie. HCR-7.<br/><br/>Lassister: Unitatea de Vest: Am dezhidratat această unitate după 2 ani de deținere a apei de inundații din 2016 pentru a face față invaziei Lotus și River Bullrush pe care o întâmpinăm. Am ars toată unitatea în această primăvară şi am marcat totul pentru a pune înapoi toate Bullrush. Această unitate va rămâne uscată în acest sezon şi este planificat să fie cultivat cu porumb primăvara viitoare. Unitatea de Est: Această unitate a devenit foarte uscată în acest an și a promovat o mulțime de creștere anuală a floarea-soarelui și aproximativ jumătate din unitate a fost disk-ed, ceea ce a pus unele dintre acele plante mai mari în creștere și a promovat o vegetație mai umedă a solului. Această unitate este dependentă de scurgere și va avea nevoie de unele precipitații semnificative pentru a obține niveluri de apă vânătoare.<br/><br/>Nord-Vest: Aproape toată vegetația lemnoasă a fost îndepărtată din această unitate. Porumb a fost plantat în această zonă umedă pentru a oferi hrană rațelor și, de asemenea, pentru a hep cu problema canari de trestie care acoperea cea mai mare parte a zonei umede. Noua structură de control al apei este instalată în partea de est a zonei umede. Această zonă umedă va putea fi pompată cu noua noastră pompă de mlaștină care a fost construită la începutul acestui an. Chiriașul și-a tăiat porția de porumb în această zonă umedă. Aceasta are o apă de drept junior și depinde de fluxurile râurilor, și am experimentat inundații în această zonă umedă și vom pompa pentru a aduce nivelul apei în sus.<br/><br/>Sediul central: Această zonă umedă a fost plantată în porumb și este încă dependentă de scurgere, deoarece avem planuri de a rula în curând o conductă de la noua noastră pompă de mlaștină la mlaștina HQ, dar acest lucru nu se va face până când expansiunea mlaștinei de la sediul central va fi finalizată. Folosim agricultura ca o tehnică pentru a menține zona "curățată" de la creșterea copacilor / perii, astfel încât odată ce lucrările de extindere sunt gata să înceapă, va fi mult mai ușor. Intenționăm să începem extinderea mlaștinii HQ cât mai curând anul viitor. Toată porumbul a fost recoltat în această zonă umedă și am lăsat porumb suplimentar în Northwest Marsh. S-au adăugat plăci și reținem orice apă.<br/><br/>Mitigation: Mlaștina de atenuare a fost trasă în această primăvară și plantată aproape în întregime la porumb. Cerbii au făcut unele daune semnificative acestui porumb, cu toate acestea, există încă o cantitate bună acolo. Această practică nu a fost doar pentru a oferi hrană pentru rațe, dar pentru a ajuta la eliminarea Phragmites invazive care a început să apară în unitate. HCR-7. Numerele de păsări de apă variază foarte mult de la o zi la alta, iar condițiile meteorologice și presiunea vânătorii vor afecta numărul de păsări de apă din zonă.<br/><br/>Rang de migrare a mallard-ului = 1 (0 = fără mallards și 10 = migrație maximă)<br/><br/>11/21/2018 ( 11:00 AM )---<br/><br/>3 Duck Marsh- Ambele unități sunt pompate și pline cu apă, 70 de mallards.<br/><br/>Unitățile din Willow Marsh- Nord și Sud sunt pompate și pline. Nu au fost observate păsări de apă la momentul sondajului.<br/><br/>Sundance Marsh- Nu au fost observate păsări de apă la momentul sondajului.<br/><br/>Cove Lowman - plin de apă.<br/><br/>Locul de parcare sud pentru Slough Shoveler este la intersecția de W 317th St. & S Davies Rd. 100 de mallards și 20 de rațe necunoscute au fost observate la momentul acestui sondaj. (NOTĂ: Barcile motorizate sunt interzise)<br/><br/>Refuge- Observat păsări de apă la momentul sondajului 150 scafandri mixte, în mare parte Goldeneye. Împreună cu aproximativ 75 Canada ganso, 30 gadwall, și 25 mallards.<br/><br/>Mielul japonez și german (50/50 mix) a fost forat în toate cele 5 zone umede și este limitat din cauza condițiilor de secetă pe tot parcursul verii. Millet precum și Milo au fost plantate în benzi alternante în toate zonele umede pentru a oferi înălțimi diferite ale structurii, precum și opțiuni pentru rațe pentru a se hrăni cu vegetație umedă și cereale. Variantele alimentare o abundenta de nevertebrate ar trebui sa ofere suficienta hrana pentru ratele sa stea in jurul valorii de tot sezonul.<br/><br/>Succesul așteptat la vânătoare<br/><br/>Echitabil<br/><br/>Comentarii<br/><br/>Toți vânătorii care utilizează Melvern Wildlife Area sunt obligați să obțină un permis de vânătoare zilnic gratuit prin intermediul noului sistem electronic de permise. Vânătorii se pot înregistra acum pentru a începe să utilizeze sistemul electronic la Pentru mai multe informații(click aici). Vânătorii de păsări de apă care utilizează lacul principal sunt obligați să obțină un permis numai atunci când vânează la vest de Hoch RoadSouth Unit- Dry și vor rămâne uscați pentru sezoanele 2018-2019 pentru lucrări de renovare (vezi "Știri Zona").<br/><br/>Pentru mai multe informații despre condițiile nivelului apei pentru sezonul următor, vă rugăm să consultați știrile din zonă făcând clic pe link-ul de mai jos și făcând clic pe pictograma "NOUȚI".<br/><br/>Condiții de vânătoare<br/><br/>- Bine.<br/><br/>Acum, piscinele sunt destul de pline pentru a rula atât în afara bordurilor, cât și motoarele de noroi în piscinele 1, 2A și 2B. Nu există restricții de motor pe zonă, doar să fii politicos cu alți vânători, pe măsură ce traversezi piscinele de vânătoare.<br/><br/>Vremea din această săptămână pare să ne poată îngheța sau cel puțin să avem gheață de peste 1 / 2 inch în weekend, așa că vă rugăm să fiți extrem de precauți pe măsură ce vremea și condițiile se schimbă.<br/><br/>Producția de porumb și mei a fost excepțională în acest an. Producția de semințe de sol umed a fost una dintre cele mai bune pe care le-am văzut în zonă și suntem extrem de mulțumiți. Nivelul habitatului și al apei sunt excelente pentru această perioadă a anului. Va rugam sa retineti ca este un sezon lung si vom continua sa crestem nivelul apei pe tot parcursul sezonului, oferind mai multa hrana cu fiecare etapa in sus.<br/><br/>Vă rugăm să consultați pagina de știri a zonei pentru o defalcare a tipurilor de habitate din fiecare piscină.<br/><br/>Succesul așteptat la vânătoare<br/><br/>Southeast Zone Regular Duck Season 10 noiembrie 2018- 6 ianuarie 2019 și 12-27 ianuarie 2018<br/><br/>Echitabil<br/><br/>Vânătoarea continuă să fie corectă. Va exista gheață în curs de dezvoltare mai târziu în săptămână, așa că utilizați prudență extremă cu schimbările condițiilor meteorologice. Vânătorii au avut o medie de aproximativ 2 păsări per vânător în acest sezon.<br/><br/>Încă mai avem vânători care nu au verificat sistemul iSportsman. Vă rugăm să verificați pentru a vă asigura că nu sunteți dvs. și vă rugăm să vă amintiți să verificați până la 11:59 zilnic. Vă mulțumesc!!!<br/><br/>Comentarii<br/><br/>Dacă semnul roșu de check out este la stația de control, toți vânătorii sunt obligați să se oprească.<br/><br/>Neosho WA cere tuturor vânătorilor să utilizeze sistemul de permise zilnice numit isportsman. Vânătorii vor trebui să se înregistreze prin intermediul PC-ului înainte de a vâna la Neosho WA. Vânătorii vor putea să se conecteze prin intermediul PC-ului, smartphone-ului sau să sune de pe telefonul mobil sau fix în fiecare zi înainte de vânătoare pentru a obține un permis zilnic și să raporteze în mod similar permiselor zilnice pe hârtie. Vă rugăm să vizitați fiecare vânător individual trebuie să verifice / afară și să raporteze totalul recoltei sale.<br/><br/>Vânătoarea de păsări de apă în Pool 8 este restricționată la vânătorii de tineri și mentorul lor în weekenduri și sărbători. |
| >4. Make friends/Surround yourself with smart people.: Not extremely practical unless you are a famous VC but even then not trivial.<br/><br/>I think it's quite practical to surround yourself with smart people without being a famous VC. I've done it. it might even be easier without being famous, unless by smart, you mean "Smart, tall and well-spoken" or "Smart, tall, and able to hold the right kind of conflicting ideas in your head without emotional distress" - which is often what VC seem to mean by smart. I mean, don't get me wrong, a lot of the ycombinater kids I've met really seemed pretty smart, but they also had the social bits you need to be good business people, and if you demand those social bits along with the 'smart' - and you should, if you are looking for business people and not just technical people - those people become dramatically more difficult to find, and when you do find them, they're difficult to be friends with, as you said, unless you are very high status, because they are very high status.<br/><br/>If you just want someone with a high G, though, and are okay with people who are short or shy or fat or who have emotional issues, as you should be if you are looking for people who can become good technical people, G alone does not confer much by way of status in this society. I've hired more than one person who tests two standard deviations better than I do by that measure. hell I've dated a few people who test two standard deviations better than I do.<br/><br/>Now, PG, I imagine, when talking about making investment decisions, probably is talking about "vc smart" - people who are smart, but also good looking, who have good attitudes and who have the other business person attributes, so in the context of this article, you're probably right. but I'm just saying, smart people usually aren't high status people. It's not hard to get smart friends, if you are willing to skimp on some of the status-granting attributes.<br/><br/>What makes you think height is so important? I have not noticed any correlation. One of the nice things about startups vs politics is that appearance is much less important for success. (unless you think Bill Gates is some kind of super model :)<br/><br/>The relationship between height and income is very well documented, at least for men.<br/><br/>It's not absolute, certainly, we can all come up with the names of successful short men, just like we can come up with the names of successful black men and successful women. It doesn't mean those things don't put you at a serious disadvantage when talking to VC or trying to get a job.<br/><br/>I started this conversation, a couple messages back, in response to this quote from sytelus:<br/><br/>>4. Make friends/Surround yourself with smart people.: Not extremely practical unless you are a famous VC but even then not trivial.<br/><br/>Which I read as someone claiming that it is difficult to become friends with high-G individuals, unless you are a high-status individual.<br/><br/>My argument was that G, in and of itself, does not confer status, and thus high-G individuals who aren't high-status aren't going to have any more people competing to be their friends than people of similar status who are more average.<br/><br/>If you want smart friends, go find them. There isn't much competition. That was my point.<br/><br/>I don't think you took issue with any of that. I think the problem was that I said:<br/><br/>>unless by smart, you mean "Smart, tall and well-spoken" or "Smart, tall, and able to hold the right kind of conflicting ideas in your head without emotional distress" - which is often what VC seem to mean by smart.<br/><br/>And you taking offense to this, really, is pretty reasonable. I'd take it as an insult if someone claimed people like me hired on irrelevant personal biases, too.<br/><br/>One way to make this seem less insulting is to say that I lump in the "tall and well spoken and able to hold the right kind of conflicting ideas in your head" with "social and business skills" - because in the work world, this has been my experience.<br/><br/>With these kinds of advice (entrepreneurial / life), you just take the best lessons and move on.<br/><br/>I like your point by point analysis. But I guess you cannot look at such articles solely through the lens of analysis.<br/><br/>On an unrelated note - I know a lot of successful professionals and entrepreneurs (mostly non tech and some tech). Surprisingly they are not that analytical as one would expect. I realized at some point in my career that great intellectual / analytical ability is just one aspect required for a successful career and there were several other important skills / traits.<br/><br/>Warren Buffet famously said (I think in reference to stock analysts who pore through and analyze stocks endlessly)<br/><br/>"Wall Street is the only place that people ride to in a Rolls Royce to get advice from those who take the subway."<br/><br/>Thanks for pointing this out. Even though I read Kahneman's "Thinking fast and slow", I did not truly understand that intelligence and rationality are orthogonal concepts. I've always implicitly assumed that intelligence is strongly correlated with rationality, but it seems there is a mild correlation at best: " it is a mild predictor at best, and some rational thinking skills are totally dissociated from intelligence"[0]<br/><br/>Yeah, at the risk of being "middle-brow", I didn't really learn much about "How to be an expert" from this.<br/><br/>Maybe, it's just a title issue, and "How to be among the first investors to spot an exploitable change" or something similar would have set my expectations properly, but I was hoping to get some useful tips that would apply to a significantly broader definition of "expert".<br/><br/>I am reminded of a interview with Amy Hoy, where she said she studied successful entrepreneurs in software "but not what they said, which was usually "work hard" but what they did."<br/><br/>I think we are expecting a lot of pg here - to be a successful entrepreneur, gain insight into what made him successful, write about it, become a successful VC and now we want a second honest and well written set of searing insights?<br/><br/>I think once was impressive enough. It is us who need to do a bit of the insight gathering now.<br/><br/>Coincidentally went to an effectual entrepreneurship workshop by Prof. Sarasvathy y'day.<br/><br/>Her learnings from talking to 'expert entrepreneurs' are that<br/><br/>1. they do not believe the future can be predicted, esp in the early stages of a new venture<br/><br/>2. they prefer to create the future in areas with high uncertainty by forging partnerships with people they know already, and who are also investing in the same areas<br/><br/>3. they go to the customer at a very low 'acceptable loss' as they can, building as little of the product as they can, investing as little as they can, and getting the first initial set of customers as partners.<br/><br/>4. they jump in to a fast iteration of execution/sales that helps them discover/create the market, relying on sales as market research.<br/><br/>5. they find pleasant surprises along the way that shape their execution/market/venture and they change to fit the new reality - and they shape that reality along the way<br/><br/>Almost certainly elderly scientists in particular; if he meant a general statement then the juxtaposition with the previous sentence is very odd. Also there's this (paraphrased from memory) quote in Rendezvous with Rama: "even after hundreds of years, science never solved the problem of having elderly, conservative scientists at the helm of its governing bodies".<br/><br/>An elderly scientist is often one who did all his work in an old theoretical paradigm, a paradigm whose death is eagerly awaited by all the young PhDs looking for jobs with a new paradigm in their thesis.<br/><br/>It really isn't a paradox. Your strength being 'always right' is a strength, but relying on it without proof is a weakness. The scientific method delivers an expert to their position, but it also keeps one in their place.<br/><br/>We always need to show our work. The fact that an expert can crutch on the Markovian Weather doesn't mean I should trust them in any way based on their word. Trust me, says the expert, I am always right. There are those who are right and there are those who are right with proof.<br/><br/>I am noticing a trend in Paul's essays. The last two were about changing paradigms. The first was about how a second read of a book leads to a different understanding, and this essay is about not letting your previous beliefs close your mind about things that can change.<br/><br/>I like though how he circles back to two fundamentals. One, human nature doesn't really change, and Two, betting on people is a better indicator of the future than betting on ideas.<br/><br/>Probably the biggest mistake people make is looking for confirming evidence instead of using evidence to determine the relative likelihood of competing hypotheses.<br/><br/>Even a large body of confirming evidence doesn't prove anything because that same evidence could be consistent with other hypotheses you never considered. You need to evaluate the diagnosticity of that evidence. (i.e. a patients high fever has almost no diagnostic value in determining what is wrong, because so many illnesses are consistent with that evidence.<br/><br/>Whereas, one shred of evidence can disprove a hypothesis completely. Which is why good doctors rule out things to identify the most likely problem.<br/><br/>These ideas PG is talking about are, IMO, some of the most important things for founders to understand.<br/><br/>Don't look to confirm your theories, look to disprove them. Don't accept lore, challenge everything. Most importantly though, always evaluate competing hypothesis to prevent you from just trying to confirm what you are looking for.<br/><br/>I use this framework, though learnt it by reading Fenmayn's writings, when there's a debate as to use tool A or tool B to get X. And it's been very effective so far. I have a vague sense that B is better and a colleague is particular about A. Earlier I used to look deeper and deeper into how B is good at doing X. But realized that I wasn't convincing enough. Then I remembered Feynman's saying to the effect "the best way to prove something is to try very hard to disprove it". This has helped quite a lot in coming up with objective comparisons and use right tool for the task at hand; or in general to come up with the right solution the problem at hand.<br/><br/>@pmarca has an interesting thread on this on Twitter [1]. In Peter Thiel's terminology [2], PG's view is optimistic nondeterminate, which might be fine for a VC but might not be the best choice for a founder.<br/><br/>Unfortunately Thiel's views don't really map to a "hard" 2x2 matrix--the axes should be considered to represent spectra or gradients instead. (I'm not presuming to be smarter than Thiel here, just extrapolating rationally... nobody believes the future to be entirely determinate or entirely indeterminate.)<br/><br/>In light of that, I suspect PG and Thiel actually live in the same quadrant of determinate optimism. I haven't read PG suggest that we should just "wait" for the future to happen--he's merely suggesting that the most common path toward building the future is on a wave. (And Thiel built PayPal that way--he recognized a version of the future that was on the cusp of being realized and took it upon himself to make it happen.)<br/><br/>Kahneman attributes a lot to 'luck' of what experts achieve especially when it comes to predicting something. I am a bit surprised that PG does not give 'shear luck' its fair share.<br/><br/>I am assuming that many (most?) of the startup founders, when they are being interviewed by the potential investors, are 'complete strangers'. By focusing objectively on ideas than subjectively or intuitively on people doing them, they believe that they mitigate the so-called risk. I am not convinced that by judging people rather than by judging ideas (in supposedly short amount of time) the chances of succeeding go up, because if we are often wrong judging ideas, what makes us good at judging people who are ever changing too?<br/><br/>Of course, there is an undeniable 'credit history' part wherein if a 'successful' founder comes back with another idea, many investors are ready to 'shower money' on her/him -- I don't find anything majorly wrong with that attitude, but that alone does not guarantee success, I believe.<br/><br/>That may be one of the very few context where judging people is easier than ideas.<br/><br/>Ideas that grow huge look completely random, except when you happen to know the one or two details that make them promising. And you can never know if you know all of those details.<br/><br/>Almost all people that make something big share some few treats that most people lack, what makes this a useful filter. Of course people can change, what reduces the effectiveness of the filter, but it's still better than random.<br/><br/>I work with making technology teams deliver faster. It's nothing as complex and tricky as what Paul's doing, but I also get to see lots of teams and make correlations between people and performance.<br/><br/>One of the things I've noticed is that over a long period of time, the more arrogant and certain team members are, the less likely they are to deliver. Folks that are always learning have a tendency to kick ass. Not so much with folks who think they've already arrived. I read in an article on HN a few weeks back that Google calls this "intellectual humility" and looks for it as a hiring trait.<br/><br/>One of the essential tensions of being a software developer is the fact that software isn't really useful on its own. It has to do something. That thing it's doing is the domain. Whatever very-specific information that the software system is organizing and passing, more specifically.<br/><br/>For example, I maintain an e-commerce system. More specifically, I maintain an e-commerce system built for a cosmetics company. E-commerce is a field, cosmetics is a field, cosmetics e-commerce is specifically the domain I need to become an expert in. The more I can differentiate cosmetics e-commerce from other kinds of e-commerce, the more well-tailored I can make my app. And the more well-tailored I can make this app, the better I'll be able to understand the more general case of making custom e-commerce systems for other industries.<br/><br/>The more I go through this process of understanding the specific needs of the industry, the more domain expertise I'm building. Domains can subdivide as much as industries subdivide. Say the cosmetic industry started to split along product lines, then I might start gaining insight into how lipstick companies operate, and be able to tailor a product offering straight to them.<br/><br/>The domain doesn't have to mean commercial industry. You might have domain expertise, say, in XML parsing. Then you could wax poetic on the differences between Nokogiri and say, Chrome's internal parser.<br/><br/>So, essentially, it just means "expertise". More specifically, the type of expertise that allows you to customize a software system around it. Which can be in practice quite different from normal expertise. The guy who devises our makeup formulas is definitely an expert.<br/><br/>But he can't design the system to actually manufacture it. The guy who writes those systems might not understand the actual formulas, so the two types of experts have to work together.<br/><br/>When startups talk about the need to be a domain expert, it means getting out of just pure programming and into the actual meat. Rather than hire an expert, to become one yourself. This not only allows you to reduce staff, it also makes it easier to make better, more custom solutions. The Soylent guys have to make the system to manufacture their product, but they also must understand nutrition, food, the regulatory environment of the food market.<br/><br/>I really feel this. As a founder, my biggest strength is that I'm a domain expert. Right now, I'm doing coding because I don't have money to hire someone else to do it, and I'm perfectly capable of it. But I feel it's not a good use of my time. Any decent programmer could do what I do most of the time right now. What they can't do is understand the domain as intimately as I do.<br/><br/>So as soon as there's money to hire programmers, I'm getting out of the coding biz and hiring others to do it. I want to focus on working with my customers, understanding their needs better than I already do, and guiding programmers to build for those needs.<br/><br/>This not only allows you to reduce staff, it also makes it easier to make better, more custom solutions.<br/><br/>I would even go as far to argue some level of domain expertise is almost required to make solutions. Even with perfect requirements (oxymoron I know), a programmer still has to make a ton of tiny domain driven decisions while writing a piece of software.<br/><br/>As you mentioned, software on its own isn't very useful. A programmer who learns even general business is much more useful.<br/><br/>I really dig this post. As it happens, the blending of domain expertise and software skills is a big reason I moved into devops. I wanted to make my domain expertise as portable as I could, while relying heavily on my software skills. Your cosmetics e-commerce app scales according to the same principles as most others', using the same techniques. Which is cool, because it increases the amount of useful knowledge in my head and allows me to apply it to a lot of different problems. (I call my role "being a multiplier", and it's a lot of fun.)<br/><br/>A set of disciplines, or majors, other than computer-science (or alternatively something other than core programming/software-engineering skills). The word is typically used in CS/sofware-engineering circles.<br/><br/>An (arguably crude) example:<br/><br/>Think of who designed an mp3 encoder/decoder? A programmer? Does becoming a good programmer and/or getting a typical 4 year CS degree, sufficient to come up with an audio encoder/decoder?<br/><br/>No. You have to understand audio signals, their properties, learn how to analyze them, etc. That requires knowledge from the disciplines of physics, and electrical engineering (e.g., digital signal processing), maybe even some biology of sound. And if you learn that, you become a part-programmer, part-acoustic-engineer. That "acoustic-engineer" bit is your "domain expertise", i.e., something you need to know beyond CS to get your work done.<br/><br/>You might say, "why do I have be an acoustic engineer? why can't I, as a programmer, work with another guy, who is not a programmer but an expert acoustic engineer?". Yes you can. In that case, you're being the software guy, and he's being the domain-expert.<br/><br/>Sometimes collaboration between a software-engineer and a domain-expert works. Sometimes it helps if the software-engineer himself develops domain-expertise (so one guy playing two roles).<br/><br/>Domain expertise is not limited to one domain only. It could be no-domain (a pure CS programming, like database, web-development etc, although you could call them domains within CS), or a domain consisting of multiple disciplines (audio-processing comprising physics, DSP), or multiple domains (acoustic-engineering plus submarine-engineering, for example), and so on.<br/><br/>There is an additional attribute that goes a long way in staying an expert in a changing world - humility or acknowledging that you do not or cannot know the future. PG hints at this when he says.. "...admit you have no idea what the right direction is, and try instead to be super sensitive to the winds of change...". It is also paraphrased quite well in the maxim of "Stay hungry, stay foolish" popularized by Jobs.<br/><br/>But humility that stems from ignorance is at odds with popular expectations of an expert. The problem is what worked in the past may not work in the future. Being sensitive to changes in the world around you, requires you to momentarily stop being an expert and look at the world from the eyes of a novice or beginner. This is not only hard but sometimes impossible to do because of how we change as we acquire new knowledge. [2]<br/><br/>So the trick is to be somewhat schizophrenic - being able to simultaneously view the world as an expert with the fresh thinking of a beginner or a novice.<br/><br/>This assumes that the goal is to be Jobs, and I completely agree with the assessment that you need to be schizophrenic. I would like to point out, the expectation is to be detail oriented(execution/expert) while leading a larger org macro goal (learning/strategy). In the right contexts with appropriate resources, this can & does magnify personalities and outputs!<br/><br/>> So the trick is to be somewhat schizophrenic - being able to simultaneously view the world as an expert with the fresh thinking of a beginner or a novice.<br/><br/>You already mentioned this, but this is extremely at odds with how experts are valued, especially by non-experts. People want answers -- not necessarily painful truths -- and ostensibly that's what they pay for. "What do you mean you're not sure? You're supposed to be an expert!"<br/><br/>This trick might work when you're a Jobs figure (or a Paul Graham, or a YC company) and you don't have non-experts to answer to. For everyone else, staying hungry and foolish means you'll be seen as hungry and foolish, and the market values you as a hungry fool, not an expert.<br/><br/>> There is an additional attribute that goes a long way in staying an expert in a changing world - humility or acknowledging that you do not or cannot know the future.<br/><br/>Sadly there's one rather important setting where this does not work at all :<br/><br/>Interviewing with a non-technical interviewer. Like one has to do for pretty much any non-individual contributor job at all but the very largest companies.<br/><br/>I keep having this impulse and I find it actually convinces people you don't know what you're talking about. The more algorithms you've seen fail spectacularly at prediction, the more capable you are (especially if you figured out why), but the less capable you appear.<br/><br/>Furthermore people always believe they can do better by just looking at the data and going with their gut feeling. This is not true, I've only seen people do that in a few cases. And I've seen people screw it up royally in more than a few cases. I think the successes were lucky guesses, because while they looked like they knew what they were doing, I could never get a decent response to the simple question : your arguments work just as well for 5 other predictions one might make, so why this one ? So I'm forced to doubt there. Either they knew more than they were willing to admit (one was also a political figure, so ...), or they were lucky guesses.<br/><br/>And yet the truly successful people have gotten to where they are by sticking to their beliefs:<br/><br/>- Warren Buffet has always believed that companies should be valued based on their profits, not their share price performance. Thus he didn't invest in the tech bubble of the 90s, and was forced to under-perform the market significantly for many years whilst everyone thought he was getting too old for the new way of things. Yet he stuck to his beliefs, and it turned out that he was right - the Nasdaq bubble evaporated and he again out-performed other funds in the long term.<br/><br/>- Steve Jobs always stuck to his belief that computing devices for consumers should be fully integrated and beautifully designed. After getting kicked out of Apple and loosing the OS war to MS, he could have easily told himself his approach was wrong. But whether he is right or wrong became irrelevant - his belief was so powerful that it was contagious.<br/><br/>- Zuck, Brin, and others have succeeded because they stuck to their beliefs instead of selling out early on.<br/><br/>- Darwin stuck to his unorthodox beliefs until his theories became accepted.<br/><br/>All of these people have/had a belief - a model of the universe which they passionately believed in. Having such a model means that predicting the future becomes possible, even though one can't predict exactly when that future will come, the same way Warren Buffet will never tell you what the market will do tomorrow, yet he certainly has a belief in where things are going long term. Jobs was too early with the Newton, yet his continued belief caused him to strike gold later on with the iPhone.<br/><br/>There is an interesting difference between fundamental beliefs and "expert knowledge". The later constantly needs to adapt to take new information into account, but it is our fundamental beliefs that determine how we interpret that expert knowledge.<br/><br/>For what it's worth, my startup investment belief has always been to: invest in companies that are run by great people that have created the best product in a growing market that has profitable competitors. I'll leave it up to the "experts" to speculate on which markets will grow most, or which competitors will win out. If Warren Buffet can't do it, I won't bother either. All that we can really judge to the best of our ability is the quality of the product and the team.<br/><br/>> And yet the truly successful people have gotten to where they are by sticking to their beliefs<br/><br/>There are a great many "startup mantras" which seem contradictory at a glance:<br/><br/>1. Founders should insist on product excellence, but release early (be embarrassed by the first version).<br/><br/>2. Startups only die when their founders give up, but founders who don't cut their losses at the right time lose credibility.<br/><br/>3. [The one at hand...] The best founders are driven by a secret about the world that other people don't know yet, and yet good founders are never married to an idea.<br/><br/>None of those things are actually contradictory, though:<br/><br/>1. An MVP is not a shitty product, it is a minimal product (founders should be embarrassed by lack of features, but the ones that are there should work well).<br/><br/>2. Both statements are true, even if uncomfortable. Add to the list of difficult balances founders must achieve.<br/><br/>3. Both statements are true here, too. I suspect that the best founders are actually driven by a family of secrets ("hypotheses" in the essay at hand) and yet would not think twice about adapting them in the face of new evidence. (Your example of Darwin is a good one to consider here--he stuck to his beliefs because he had strong evidence for them, and he would have changed his beliefs had new evidence presented itself. The prevailing opinion of society did not factor into that process.)<br/><br/>I think PG's point conflates a whole bunch of unrelated stuff, including:<br/><br/>1. Experts as people with unusual technical competence in one or more related fields.<br/><br/>2. Experts as 'big picture' prophets of the future.<br/><br/>3. Experts as 'small picture' refiners of future tech.<br/><br/>4. Experts as packagers, innovators, and marketers who create new markets for the work of 2 and 3.<br/><br/>5. Experts as investors who look for all of the above and try to work out if they can make money from them.<br/><br/>Aren't these mostly unrelated job descriptions? There's no reason why a brilliant academic who understands compiler design would have anything useful to say about the future of consumer electronics.<br/><br/>Likewise, I wouldn't have expected Steve Jobs to contribute much to (say) research into room temperature semiconductors. Steve Jobs was brilliant at packaging and selling stuff, not at theoretical physics.<br/><br/>PG's real point seems to be that if you 'hire' smart, creative, hard-working people, they're more likely to do something useful for you than people who aren't smart, creative, or hard-working.<br/><br/>Which is surely true (if they're not difficult) but doesn't have much to do with expert predictions about the future.<br/><br/>It's kind of a retread of Spolsky's 'smart, gets things done', with a more entrepreneurial twist and an implied 'has original insights'. (Spolsky being an excellent example of all four.)<br/><br/>> If you're sufficiently expert in a field, any weird idea or apparently irrelevant question that occurs to you is ipso facto worth exploring.<br/><br/>My proudest creations all came from these weird ideas. Now I listen to them very carefully and nurture them. They're starting to cohere and turn from random ideas into an ideology. It will be fun to make that real.<br/><br/>Updating one's beliefs to get more accurate priors is something we should all strive to do. There is no such thing as having two beliefs of equal weight that contradict each other - that's an illusion created by either applying a different definitions to each belief, or a refusal to admit that you believe one more than the other.<br/><br/>...<br/><br/>> "The winds of change originate in the unconscious minds of domain experts."<br/><br/>I would also add that domain expertise is when one's internal model / map matches the territory. When this happens, one's intuition is accurate and one can notice patterns that other people can't, or one can make predictions that turn out to be right.<br/><br/>>There is no such thing as having two beliefs of equal weight that contradict each other - that's an illusion created by either applying a different definitions to each belief, or a refusal to admit that you believe one more than the other.<br/><br/>There's definitely a risk as a domain expert that you'll take things for granted. Henry Ford is a good example of this, he was never an expert in horse-drawn carriages but he changed that domain drastically.<br/><br/>Great read. I would go a bit further and say that the goal is not to be an expert and is not related to start-ups. The goal of being open minded and sceptical as described in this essay, is to make sure one do not embrace sheepishly some ideas that will be considered obviously stupid twenty years in the future. For example in the 50s in Europe most intellectuals were communist or pro communist. Only a very few were neither reactionary nor communist. The goal, for me, is to be of these few. In the US history, it would mean to have been e.g.among the earliest abolitionists.<br/><br/>There's a subtle theme in this essay which describes the thought process of how YC (well pg specifically) selects teams for YC. Perhaps in a way, bringing clarity to what is generally seen as a non-scientific and highly complex line of reasoning for hiring and selecting talent with very little limited context. However, I still don't feel like I've still fully grokked it.<br/><br/>> Within Y Combinator, when an idea is described as crazy, it's a compliment—in fact, on average probably a higher compliment than when an idea is described as good.<br/><br/>Yet, outside the walls of YC, sounding crazy means you don't get in.<br/><br/>> But we could tell the founders were earnest, energetic, and independent-minded. What made YC take the risk on Chesky, Gebbia, and Blecharczyk?<br/><br/>Determination seems to be one decisive trait. If you're determined, people often want to help you succeed or to work with you. Determination also seems to matter more than raw intelligence, and betting on determined people tends to be a good bet.<br/><br/>Being willing to work for low payoff seems to be one decisive trait. If you're willing to work for low payoff, people often want to take advantage of you. Being a sucker also seems to matter more than raw intelligence, and betting on suckers tends to be a good bet.<br/><br/>But a population is much slower. To the point where "human nature doesn't change" is an extremely useful heuristic. If you look for counter examples, you'll find a few changes that took only a century or two, but in practice you'll probably never see one of those changes.<br/><br/>Old people in general are such experts, or some "fundamentalists" or "conservatives" - those, who are trying to "preserve" the world they grow up in, everyone who follow and worship a "ritual", a "process", stuck up "creationists" or "froidians", "public static void" guys - the list is surprisingly long.<br/><br/>Some "teachers" said millennias ago "be the change", others said that "we are never the same". Perhaps, they have tried to capture this notion of "an ever changing process" - us (we are processes, not entities, they said) in a "beginningless and endless change" which they call "That" or "Tao", and we call Universe.<br/><br/>ASK PG: when you are saying "energetic" people, do you also mean "passionately convinced"?<br/><br/>It's interesting that on one side you try to avoid the pitfalls of your own beliefs (by smartly adopting meta-beliefs and techniques that sidestep the problem, like believing in change and focusing on people)<br/>but at the same time you are suggesting that you somehow accept, or decide to trust, the worldview of "energetic" founders.<br/><br/>You might not mean it, but this connection is somehow untold in the classical narrative of the driven founder: there is a fine line between "energetic" and "passionately convinced", which also suggests that they might have and communicate strong beliefs in their success or their idea.<br/>Of course I understand that "strong beliefs" aren't forcefully connected with being an expert, which is your main argument.<br/><br/>PG, Where do you stand on this? do you believe in founder's beliefs? Do founders who passionately believe in something convince you?<br/><br/>I feel very sympathetic to the argument of your essay, for many reasons, and have grown to use the same meta-belief of believing in change; however, instead of sidestepping the problem, I tackle it heads on, by incorporating Doubt and Relativism in my decision making.<br/>I feel, and strongly believe ;), that Relativism is the cure to many cognitive biases; if you doubt yourself you can't be affected by the Dunning-Kruger effect; I think everybody should train themselves in managing the unavoidable cognitive dissonances of day to day life, it would make the world a better place. Relativism is also an important tool for creativity: the moment you doubt your frame of reference, you start seeing the picture outside of the box, new possibilities open up.<br/><br/>Of course Relativism has it's shortcomings, and beliefs, which are rooted in emotions, command our behaviours more powerfully that the logical reasonings of Relativism.<br/>However, I believe, there are ways for us to be both "passionately convinced" and relativistic at the same time;<br/>I try to be like Cezanne, who would ask himself: "is this what I see?"<br/><br/>I wonder if being open minded is enough. The "winds of change" aren't always very strong, and accordingly new opportunities aren't (at least initially) better alternatives to the status quo, they just happen to have incredible future potential. To fully take advantage of this, the expert would have to move backwards, quite possibly endangering himself, while a new entrant to the field has no risk to assume by doing so.<br/>To use the hill-climbing metaphor described by other commenters, a new climber can begin climbing the new potentially tall mountain immediately, while the "expert" will first have to descend his peak.<br/><br/>> The winds of change originate in the unconscious minds of domain experts. If you're sufficiently expert in a field, any weird idea or apparently irrelevant question that occurs to you is ipso facto worth exploring.<br/><br/>I frequently wrangle with challenges related to finding other people who are open to listening and helping me explore these less-than-complete ideas in an unbiased way. It's always an enjoyable moment when someone listens and then adds to the conversation, instead of immediately trying to negate the kernel of the idea based on the fact it's in a chaotic state at the time.<br/><br/>Talking about non-conform ideas, I would like to add that what should be of interest is not what changed but what doesn't. And I would like to offer that this idea of a world of accelerated change might in fact be a self-serving, Ptolemaic fantasy of omnipotence.<br/>What counts hasn't changed much in centuries. We're still using steam engines to produce electricity. And society is very similar to what it was in the Bronze Age.<br/><br/>"It seems to me that beliefs about the future are so rarely correct that they usually aren't worth the extra rigidity they impose, and that the best strategy is simply to be aggressively open-minded.
